2603.16185.title: Sample-Efficient Adaptation of Drug-Response Models to Patient Tumors under Strong Biological Domain Shift
2603.16185.paper_summary: Predicting drug response in patients from preclinical data remains a major challenge in precision oncology due to the substantial biological gap between in vitro cell lines and patient tumors. Rather than aiming to improve absolute in vitro prediction accuracy, this work examines whether explicitly separating representation learning from task supervision enables more sample-efficient adaptation of drug-response models to patient data under strong biological domain shift. We propose a staged transfer-learning framework in which cellular and drug representations are first learned independently from large collections of unlabeled pharmacogenomic data using autoencoder-based representation learning. These representations are then aligned with drug-response labels on cell-line data and subsequently adapted to patient tumors using few-shot supervision. Through a systematic evaluation spanning in-domain, cross-dataset, and patient-level settings, we show that unsupervised pretraining provides limited benefit when source and target domains overlap substantially, but yields clear gains when adapting to patient tumors with very limited labeled data. In particular, the proposed framework achieves faster performance improvements during few-shot patient-level adaptation while maintaining comparable accuracy to single-phase baselines on standard cell-line benchmarks. Overall, these results demonstrate that learning structured and transferable representations from unlabeled molecular profiles can substantially reduce the amount of clinical supervision required for effective drug-response prediction, offering a practical pathway toward data-efficient preclinical-to-clinical translation.
2603.16185.paper_summary_zh: 預測患者的藥物反應從前臨床數據中仍然是精準腫瘤學的一大挑戰，因為體外細胞株與患者腫瘤之間存在顯著的生物學差距。這項工作並不旨在提高絕對的體外預測準確性，而是考察明確將表示學習與任務監督分開是否能夠在強生物領域轉移下更有效地適應藥物反應模型到患者數據。我們提出了一個分階段的轉移學習框架，在這個框架中，細胞和藥物的表示首先通過基於自編碼器的表示學習，從大量未標記的藥物基因組數據中獨立學習。然後，這些表示與細胞株數據上的藥物反應標籤對齊，並隨後使用少量監督適應到患者腫瘤。通過系統的評估，涵蓋了域內、跨數據集和患者級別的設置，我們顯示當源域和目標域重疊顯著時，無監督的預訓練提供的好處有限，但在適應到標記數據非常有限的患者腫瘤時卻帶來了明顯的增益。特別是，所提出的框架在少量患者級別適應期間實現了更快的性能改進，同時在標準細胞株基準上保持與單階段基線相當的準確性。總體而言，這些結果表明，從未標記的分子特徵中學習結構化和可轉移的表示可以顯著減少有效藥物反應預測所需的臨床監督量，為數據高效的前臨床到臨床轉換提供了一條實際途徑。
2603.16185.author: Camille Jimenez Cortes et.al.
2603.16185.authors: Camille Jimenez Cortes, Philippe Lalanda, German Vega
